front: Figure: formed the First Triumvirate with Marcus Licinius Crassus and Julius Caesar
work: General / Giveaway Identifiers

front: Figure: Cicero praised him for having military knowledge, prestige, excellence, and luck in a speech supporting the Lex Gabinia
work: General / Giveaway Identifiers

front: Figure: married Julia, the daughter of his rival Julius Caesar, as part of a political alliance
work: General / Giveaway Identifiers

front: Figure: Plutarch paired his biography with that of the Spartan king Agesilaus II
work: General / Giveaway Identifiers

front: Figure: had two sons, Gnaeus (named after him) and Sextus, the latter of whom later rebelled against Octavian in Sicily
work: General / Giveaway Identifiers

front: Event: earned the nickname "the Young Butcher" after executing the consul Carbo and his supporters during Sulla's civil war
work: Early Military Career: Sulla's Civil War and the Sertorian War

front: Event: became the only member of the equestrian class ever awarded a Triumph, first receiving one after his African victories, which also earned him the epithet "Magnus"
work: Early Military Career: Sulla's Civil War and the Sertorian War

front: War: worked alongside Quintus Caecilius Metellus Pius to suppress the revolt of the renegade governor Sertorius in Spain, losing the battles of Lauron and Sucro before winning through a war of attrition
work: Early Military Career: Sulla's Civil War and the Sertorian War

front: Event: drove the rebellious former consul Lepidus out of Italy
work: Early Military Career: Sulla's Civil War and the Sertorian War

front: Event: angered Crassus by taking credit for ending the revolt of Spartacus, despite Crassus having done most of the fighting
work: Suppression of Spartacus and First Consulship

front: Event: served as consul alongside Crassus in 70 BC, despite never having held a lower elected office
work: Suppression of Spartacus and First Consulship

front: Law: gave Pompey unprecedented, sweeping power over the entire Mediterranean coastline to hunt down the Cilician pirates
work: Campaign Against the Cilician Pirates

front: Event: completed his campaign against the Cilician pirates in only three months despite being granted a three-year term
back: Pompey's campaign against the Cilician pirates
work: Campaign Against the Cilician Pirates

front: Battle: Pompey's decisive victory over the Cilician pirates, marking the turning point of his 67 BC campaign
work: Campaign Against the Cilician Pirates

front: Event: divided the Mediterranean coasts into thirteen districts under subordinate legates during his anti-piracy campaign, but personally overrode Metellus's authority to accept the pirates' surrender on Crete
back: Pompey's campaign against the Cilician pirates
work: Campaign Against the Cilician Pirates

front: Law: transferred command of the war against Mithridates VI and Tigranes the Great from the ineffectual Lucius Licinius Lucullus to Pompey
work: War Against Mithridates and the Eastern Settlement

front: Event: ultimately defeated Mithridates VI of Pontus in the last of his three wars against Rome
work: War Against Mithridates and the Eastern Settlement

front: Event: cultivated an anastole hairstyle in imitation of Alexander the Great to bolster his image as Alexander's successor
work: War Against Mithridates and the Eastern Settlement

front: Event: annexed Syria as a Roman province and incorporated Judaea into Rome's sphere of influence during his eastern settlement
work: War Against Mithridates and the Eastern Settlement

front: Event: became governor of Hispania Ulterior while remaining in Rome with his new wife Julia, part of the informal alliance now called the First Triumvirate
work: First Triumvirate and Political Life in Rome

front: Event: was devastated by his wife Julia's death, made worse by her burial in the Campus Martius rather than his favorite Alban estate
work: First Triumvirate and Political Life in Rome

front: Event: controlled Rome's grain supply for five years and won a hotly contested consulship opposed by Ahenobarbus, who objected to his awarding Caesar the proconsulship of Gaul
work: First Triumvirate and Political Life in Rome

front: Event: was appointed sole consul to quell the riots caused by Publius Clodius Pulcher's street gangs after Clodius's death in 52 BC
work: First Triumvirate and Political Life in Rome

front: Event: arranged the consulship of Afranius, whose only real skill turned out to be dancing
work: First Triumvirate and Political Life in Rome

front: Event: drifted toward the anti-Caesarian Optimates faction after Crassus's death at Carrhae removed the buffer holding the First Triumvirate together
work: First Triumvirate and Political Life in Rome

front: Structure: Rome's first permanent theater, built on the Campus Martius, with a temple to Venus Victrix on its top level to evade a censorial ban on permanent theaters
work: Theater of Pompey

front: Structure: its 55 BC opening ceremonies featured the actor Clodius Aesopus and were marred by an ill-omened elephant fight
work: Theater of Pompey

front: Structure: its attached curia served as a temporary Senate meeting place in the 40s BC and was the site of Julius Caesar's assassination in 44 BC
work: Theater of Pompey

front: Battle: Pompey won this indecisive engagement, prompting Caesar to withdraw his forces, shortly before their decisive clash at Pharsalus
work: Civil War with Caesar and Death in Egypt

front: Event: debated the concept of providence with the philosopher Cratippus of Pergamon after his defeat at Pharsalus, according to Plutarch
work: Civil War with Caesar and Death in Egypt

front: Event: fled to Egypt after Pharsalus, where the eunuch Pothinus convinced the boy-king Ptolemy XIII to have him killed as he landed at Alexandria in 48 BC
work: Civil War with Caesar and Death in Egypt

front: Event: his body was cremated on a pyre by his freedman Philip
work: Civil War with Caesar and Death in Egypt

front: Event: his severed head, presented to Caesar along with his signet ring, reportedly enraged rather than pleased Caesar, who ordered Pothinus and Achillas executed
work: Civil War with Caesar and Death in Egypt

front: Event: his head was later used in the founding of a temple to Nemesis, in view of his widow Cornelia
work: Civil War with Caesar and Death in Egypt
